mnemonics.title: Sustainability Pillars
mnemonics.content: The 3 E's of Sustainability: Environment, Economics, and Equity (Social)

mnemonics.title: Green IT Metrics
mnemonics.content: PEEW: PUE (Power), CUE (Carbon), WUE (Water), REF (Renewable) - remember as 'Pure Clean Water Refreshes'

mnemonics.title: LCA Stages
mnemonics.content: Cradle to Grave: Raw Materials → Production → Distribution → Use → Disposal

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency: Total Facility Energy ÷ IT Equipment Energy
Measures carbon emissions per unit of IT energy consumed
Measures water consumption for cooling in data centers
Inverse of PUE, expressed as percentage (IT Energy ÷ Total Facility Energy)
Evaluates environmental impacts throughout a product's entire life cycle
International framework for sustainability reporting standards
Develops international standards including environmental management (14001) and energy (50001)
Discarded electronic devices requiring special disposal and recycling

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle: Center = IT Equipment Energy, Outer Ring = Total Facility Energy. PUE = Outer ÷ Center. Ideally close to 1.0
Draw an equilateral triangle with Environment (top), Economic (bottom-left), Social (bottom-right) - all equal and interconnected
Draw a linear arrow with 5 stages: Cradle → Production → Distribution → Use → Grave (Disposal). Show arrows looping back for recycling

analogies.analogy: PUE is like a car's fuel efficiency - lower is better. PUE of 1.0 means all energy goes to moving (computing), with no waste heat or idling.
Virtualization is like one person managing multiple rental properties instead of building separate houses for each tenant - saves resources and space.
Carbon footprint is like a dietary calorie count for the planet - you're tracking what 'energy' you're consuming and its environmental 'cost'.
E-waste recycling is like composting food scraps - turning waste into resources instead of filling landfills with harmful materials.
